mnemonics.title: Selection Sort Steps
mnemonics.content: MINIMUM IN UNSORTED - FIND IT AND SWAP IT TO FRONT, THEN SHRINK THE UNSORTED PART

mnemonics.title: Time Complexity
mnemonics.content: N SQUARED ALWAYS - BUBBLE INSERTION SELECTION ALL THE SAME

mnemonics.title: Swaps Property
mnemonics.content: ONLY N-1 SWAPS MAX - THIS IS WHAT MAKES SELECTION SORT SPECIAL

acronyms.expansion: Algorithm requiring only O(1) extra memory
acronyms.usage: Selection Sort is IN-PLACE sorting algorithm

acronyms.expansion: Preserves relative order of equal elements
acronyms.usage: Selection Sort is NOT STABLE unlike Bubble Sort

visualTips.visualization: Imagine scanning a row of unsorted cards to find the smallest one, then placing it at the leftmost position
Think of a divide between sorted (left) and unsorted (right) portions, with the wall moving one step right each pass
Triangle arrangement: first row has n-1 comparisons, next has n-2, forming triangle totaling n(n-1)/2

analogies.analogy: Like arranging cards in your hand: look through unsorted cards, find the smallest, place it in position, repeat
Like selecting a team captain: scan all candidates, pick the best, assign position, repeat for next position
Find the shortest book, put it first; then find shortest among remaining, put it second, continue until all arranged
